title: `JOIN` using `OR` only picks the first match
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
I noticed a breaking change between 25.7 to 25.8 which I can't spot in [the changelog](https://clickhouse.com/docs/whats-new/changelog#258).
A query using `OR` in `JOIN` seems to only return the first match when using reference. This was not the behavior in 25.7 and I don't think it's expected given that this is not how e.g. `WHERE` works.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.8
### How to reproduce
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_data (id Int32, value Int32) ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (0, 100), (1, 200), (2, 300);
```
### 25.7
```sql
SELECT
target.id AS target_id,
reference.id AS reference_id,
reference.value
FROM test_data AS target
INNER JOIN test_data AS reference ON (reference.id = (target.id - 2)) OR (reference.id = (target.id - 1)) OR (reference.id = (target.id - 0))
WHERE target.id = 2
ORDER BY reference_id ASC
Query id: 24412fd7-5941-46e7-9f8f-9f6af1332fbb
в”Ңв”Җtarget_idв”Җв”¬в”Җreference_idв”Җв”¬в”Җreference.valueв”Җв”җ
1. в”Ӯ         2 в”Ӯ            0 в”Ӯ             100 в”Ӯ
2. в”Ӯ         2 в”Ӯ            1 в”Ӯ             200 в”Ӯ
3. в”Ӯ         2 в”Ӯ            2 в”Ӯ             300 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
3 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.007 sec.
```
### 25.8
```sql
SELECT
target.id AS target_id,
reference.id AS reference_id,
reference.value
FROM test_data AS target
INNER JOIN test_data AS reference ON (reference.id = (target.id - 2)) OR (reference.id = (target.id - 1)) OR (reference.id = (target.id - 0))
WHERE target.id = 2
Query id: d9ebc614-8635-4a3b-9a09-69428291db7c
в”Ңв”Җtarget_idв”Җв”¬в”Җreference_idв”Җв”¬в”Җreference.valueв”Җв”җ
1. в”Ӯ         2 в”Ӯ            2 в”Ӯ             300 в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
1 row in set. Elapsed: 0.003 sec.
```
I suspect this is related to what `target` and/or `reference` is referring to in the `JOIN` condition since basic literals resolves as expected in both. From 25.8:
```sql
SELECT
target.id AS target_id,
reference.id AS reference_id,
poc: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_data (id Int32, value Int32) ENGINE = Memory;
key_bug_features: JOIN using OR only returns the first match
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS test_data_v2 (id Int32, value Nullable(Int32)) ENGINE = Memory;

SELECT target.id AS target_id, reference.id AS reference_id, reference.value FROM test_data_v2 AS target INNER JOIN test_data_v2 AS reference ON (reference.id = (target.id - 2)) OR (reference.id = (target.id - 1)) WHERE target.id = 2;

SELECT target.id AS target_id, reference.id AS reference_id, reference.value FROM test_data_v2 AS target INNER JOIN test_data_v2 AS reference ON (reference.value IS NULL) OR (reference.id = (target.id - 1)) WHERE target.id = 1;

SELECT target.id AS target_id, reference.id AS reference_id, reference.value FROM test_data_v2 AS target INNER JOIN test_data_v2 AS reference ON (reference.id = (target.id - 2)) OR (reference.id = (target.id - 1)) OR (reference.id = (target.id)) WHERE target.id = 0;
